title: geum macrophyllum var. sachalinense
intro: Geum macrophyllum var. sachalinense, also known as the large leaf avens or Sakhalin geum, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to the Far East, particularly in Russia and Japan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Geum macrophyllum var. sachalinense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. The plant is typically prepared for herbal use by making a tea from its roots or leaves, which can also be dried and used in infusions or as a tincture.
